activities_projects.title: Timeline of the Cherry Tree
activities_projects.objective: Understand the development of events in the story
steps: Read the story carefully
Identify important events such as planting the seed, the goat eating leaves, and the tree flowering
Arrange these events on a timeline
Present the timeline in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand chronological order and story development
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of events and presentation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Character Traits of Rakesh
activities_projects.objective: Analyse the personality traits of Rakesh
steps: Identify actions of Rakesh in the story
List qualities such as patience, curiosity, and care for nature
Write examples from the text supporting each trait
Share your findings with classmates
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of examples and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Nature and Growth Discussion
activities_projects.objective: Understand the theme of growth and perseverance
steps: Discuss how the cherry tree faced difficulties such as animals and weather
Explain how the tree continued to grow
Relate the story to real-life experiences of overcoming challenges
Write a short paragraph about the lesson learned

activities_projects.title: Cause and Effect Chart
activities_projects.objective: Identify relationships between events in the story
steps: Identify major events from the story
Write the causes and their effects
Create a chart showing these relationships
Display the chart in the classroom
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop analytical thinking and understanding of narrative structure

activities_projects.title: Dialogue Re-enactment
steps: Select a scene between Rakesh and his grandfather
Write the dialogue from the scene
Practice the dialogue with a partner
Perform the scene in front of the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop communication and performance skills

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary in Context
activities_projects.objective: Understand vocabulary used in the story
steps: List words such as 'plodded', 'scrambled', 'gazed', and 'yielding'
Find their meanings using a dictionary
Write sentences using each word
Share sentences with classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students improve vocabulary and contextual usage

activities_projects.title: Observation Journal
activities_projects.objective: Develop observation and descriptive writing skills
steps: Observe a plant or tree in your surroundings for a few days
Write daily observations about its growth or changes
Describe the environment around the plant
Present your observations in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop observation and descriptive writing abilities
activities_projects.evaluation: Completeness of journal and clarity of description (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Theme Identification
activities_projects.objective: Identify the main themes of the story
steps: Read the story again and identify themes such as patience, nature, and growth
Write short explanations for each theme
Find supporting examples from the text
Discuss the themes in class

activities_projects.title: Grammar Practice – Past Tenses
activities_projects.objective: Understand past tense forms used in the story
steps: Find sentences from the chapter written in the past tense
Identify simple past, past progressive, and past perfect forms
Rewrite the sentences in another tense form
Check answers with classmates

activities_projects.title: Reflective Writing
activities_projects.objective: Express personal thoughts about the story
steps: Think about how Rakesh felt while watching the tree grow
Imagine you planted a tree and watched it grow
Write a short reflection describing your feelings
Share your writing with the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop reflective and creative writing skills
